title: poset_phase v4.0.0: Action-Weighted Poset Ensemble Analysis — Prediction C v2: From Correlational Evidence to Quasi-Causal Intervention
description: Exact numerical framework for studying geometric phase transitions in finite partially ordered sets (posets). v4.0.0 upgrades Prediction C from correlational-only to quasi-causal evidence. Nine experiments form a layered evidence tower: (1) Stratified Fisher z regression (|r|~0.35-0.54); (2) Single-edge causal intervention (Cohen's d=1.05); (3) Placebo-controlled intervention across Lor2D/3D/4D (d=1.40-1.83, p<1e-133); (4) Reverse intervention with 100% directional consistency (d=2.68); (5) k-family dose-response (all slopes negative, p<1e-29); (6) Edge density universality (no phase transition at any p); (7) N-scaling law (|slope|∝N^0.70); (8) Analytical lower bound for complete layered posets (validated to 1e-15); (9) Large-N SIS extension to N=36 (d=0.69-1.29, p<1e-5 at all N). Manuscript: 1020 lines, 11915 words, 43 pages, 8 sections, 22 tables. Previous versions: v3.0.0 (Prediction C correlational), v2.1.0 (Prediction B bounded γ_c), v2.0.0 (Prediction A 4D Lorentzian dominance).
notes: This software accompanies three manuscripts: (1) Prediction B — 'Bounded Geometric Phase Transition in Finite Causal Posets with Exact Entropy', (2) Prediction A — 'Dimension-Agnostic Geometric Dominance in Finite Causal Posets', (3) Prediction C v2 — 'Hierarchy Depth Predicts Combinatorial Entropy in Finite Causal Posets: From Correlational Evidence to Quasi-Causal Intervention' (1020 lines, 9 experiments, 43pp PDF). AI tools (Claude, Anthropic) assisted with code development and analysis.
